data.description:
# terraform-drift check permanently red: opcert_admin trust-policy phantom drift

## Context / proven root cause
The `terraform-drift` GitHub Actions check has failed on every run against
`origin/main` since opcert.tf landed (commit f78940a43, 2026-07-15). The check runs
`terraform plan` over `infra/terraform/` with NO variable inputs
(.github/workflows/terraform-drift.yml:61). `aws_iam_role.opcert_admin`'s
assume_role_policy derives its principals from `var.opcert_admin_principal_arns`
(opcert.tf:138), which defaults to `[]` (opcert.tf:29). The live role was applied with
the operator-supplied account-root principal (`arn:aws:iam::896586841071:root`, per the
documented deploy pattern opcert.tf:26 / opcert-plan-assertions.sh:22). So every plan
renders an EMPTY principal and diffs it against live's account-root principal:

    # aws_iam_role.opcert_admin will be updated in-place
      ~ Principal = { ~ AWS = "arn:aws:iam::896586841071:root" -> [] }
    Plan: 0 to add, 1 to change, 0 to destroy.   -> exit 2 -> check FAILS

This is an un-reconcilable phantom (applying the committed default would remove the
principal), so it permanently reds the daily drift sweep and masks any real future drift.

## Defect
Asymmetry inside opcert.tf: the sibling operator-seeded SSM KEY param carries
`lifecycle { ignore_changes = [value] }` (opcert.tf:57-59) precisely so plan/apply never
diff on an operator-owned value — but the equally operator-supplied trust principal was
not given the same guard.

## Plan (fix direction: ignore_changes — user-approved)
Add `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [assume_role_policy] }` to `aws_iam_role.opcert_admin`.
Terraform still creates the role; the operator owns the trust list out-of-band (honors the
documented "operator supplies principals at deploy" intent and mirrors the key-param
precedent). This suppresses the phantom diff at PLAN time — no terraform apply required.

## Acceptance criteria (measurable)
1. `aws_iam_role.opcert_admin` in infra/terraform/opcert.tf declares
   `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [assume_role_policy] }`.
2. A hermetic source-contract test in tests/unit/test_opcert_deploy_infra.py asserts (1)
   and was seen RED before the fix, GREEN after (with a mutation check returning it to RED).
3. `terraform fmt -check` and `terraform validate` pass on the edited module.
4. No existing opcert infra test is weakened; full unit suite for the file passes.

## Verify command
    cd /Users/joeoakhart/fix-opcert-drift-ignore-changes
    ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/test_opcert_deploy_infra.py -q
    cd infra/terraform && terraform fmt -check && terraform validate
data.title: terraform-drift check permanently red: opcert_admin trust-policy phantom drift
